overview.purpose: Soupify bundles multiple source files into a single markdown file for transmission to AI systems. Your role is to deserialize (desoupify) these files, perform requested tasks, and reserialize (resoupify) the results.
workflow: Receive a soupified (.soup or .md) file containing multiple source files
Parse the block headers to reconstruct the original files
Perform the requested tasks (analysis, modifications, debugging, etc.)
Resoupify only modified/new files back into a soup file

Return the soup file: in a chat context emit it as a single fenced code block (language tag `soup` or none) so markdown will not misrender #SOUP/#SOUP_META lines as headers; with tool/disk access write it to a .md file.

soup_format_specification.description: A soup file contains zero or more meta blocks (first) followed by zero or more file blocks. Blocks concatenate directly — no blank lines between them.

components.path: Original absolute path, JSON-encoded (backslashes→\\, quotes→\"). Preserve exactly on resoupify.
components.SOUP_PARTIAL_LINES: Optional. This block replaces only lines s–e (1-indexed, inclusive) of the existing file. Omit for full-file blocks.
components.SOUPED_LINES: Exact count of content lines immediately following this header.
components.SOUP_TRAILING_NEWLINE: 1 = file ends with \n, 0 = it does not. Serializer always writes 0 or 1 (parser also accepts true/false).

meta_block_header.description: Carries machine-readable metadata (e.g. a codegraph). Generated by soupify -g (equivalent long form: --include-graph). Always appears before file blocks. Parse N lines of content then SKIP — do not write to disk. Do not emit meta blocks yourself.

desoupify.description: Convert a soup file back into the original files.
algorithm: 2. Meta block: extract SOUP_META_LINES N, consume exactly N content lines, discard — do not write.
3. File block: extract path (JSON-decode), optional partial range, SOUPED_LINES N, trailing-newline flag.
4. Consume exactly N content lines after the header.
5. Full block (no partial range): join lines with \n, append \n if flag=1. Create parent dirs. Write to disk.

6. Partial block: read target file as it currently exists on disk (reflecting any prior partial edits to it). Replace lines s–e (1-indexed) with the block's content lines. Write merged result back. If you have no tool/disk access you must already know the full content of the target file (from an earlier full block in this soup, a prior soup in the session, or your own prior writes); if none of those hold, do not guess — ask the user to resend a full block for that file.
7. Repeat until all blocks are processed.
implementation_notes: Partial blocks require the target file to exist and be long enough to cover end_line.
Multiple partial blocks on the same file are applied in document order; each sees the result of the previous.

resoupify.description: Convert modified files back into a soup file. Only emit files you modified or created.
algorithm: 1. Identify all files modified or created during your work.
2. For each file, choose full or partial output:
FULL — rewritten or new file: read content, count lines, emit header then all content lines.
PARTIAL — localized change in a large file: emit header with SOUP_PARTIAL_LINES, then replacement lines only.
3. Concatenate all blocks directly (no blank lines between) into a .md output.

line_counting.rule: Strip trailing \n if present, split remainder on \n, count segments. Empty string = 0 lines.

partial_block_notes: Trailing-newline flag reflects the WHOLE file's state after the edit, not just the replacement lines.
When emitting multiple partial blocks for the same file, line numbers in later blocks must account for line-count changes caused by earlier partial blocks.
Emit multiple partial blocks for the same file in ascending start_line order.
Choose FULL vs PARTIAL by replacement size: if replacement lines >= 50% of the file, emit a FULL block; otherwise prefer PARTIAL (even for interleaved edits).

Safety valve: if multiple partials on the same file would require complex line-shift bookkeeping (e.g. interleaved edits with large deltas), prefer a single FULL block over risky shifted partials — correctness outranks brevity.
implementation_notes: Serialize trailing newline as 0 or 1 (never true/false).
JSON-escape file paths (use JSON string serialization).
Do not re-emit unchanged files unless the user asks.
resoupify.delivery: In a chat context, emit the resoupified output as a single fenced code block with language tag `soup` (or no language) so #SOUP/#SOUP_META lines are not rendered as headers. Do not split across multiple code blocks. With tool/disk access, write to a .md file at the user's chosen path.

host_integration.auto_desoupify: #SOUP_AUTO_DESOUPIFY is NOT a tag that appears inside a soup file. Auto-desoupify is a host-side config flag (soupify auto_desoupify: true in the host's config) that registers a filesystem rule: any *.soup.md file landing in the watched downloads folder is automatically desoupified via `soupify -d __FILE__`. If you ever encounter the literal string #SOUP_AUTO_DESOUPIFY inside a soup body, treat it as ordinary file content — it is not part of this spec and carries no meaning for your parser.

practical_examples.with_meta_block:
#SOUP_META "repo-graph" #SOUP_META_KIND codegraph #SOUP_META_FORMAT repomap #SOUP_META_LINES 1 #SOUP_META_READONLY true
<graph data — skip, do not write>
#SOUP "/tmp/index.js" #SOUPED_LINES 1 #SOUP_TRAILING_NEWLINE 0
console.log('hi');

error_handling.malformed_header: Report the line number and problematic content to the user.
error_handling.line_count_mismatch: Report expected vs. actual line count and the affected file path.
error_handling.invalid_partial_range: Report if start > end, either value is 0, or end exceeds existing file length.
error_handling.missing_metadata: Report which of SOUPED_LINES or SOUP_TRAILING_NEWLINE is absent and the affected path.

best_practices: Validate soup block headers before consuming file content.
Track modifications carefully so you can resoupify accurately.
Create parent directories automatically when desoupifying.
Line counts must be exact — off-by-one errors corrupt the soup file.
Handle the trailing newline flag correctly; it is essential for exact file restoration.
Empty files: #SOUPED_LINES 0 #SOUP_TRAILING_NEWLINE 0 with no content lines.
Prefer partial blocks for large files with small changes to reduce output size.
Add any newly created files to the resoupified output even if they weren't in the original soup.
If the user requests specific files, only resoupify those files.
